steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s defining clue is the contrast word counterintuitive, which signals that the main idea hinges on an expectation being reversed. The text presents a correlation (resource-rich developing countries receive less foreign investment) and explains it as the opposite of what one might initially expect.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice C captures the reversed expectation: it notes that one might expect investment to increase with resource reliance and explains that it instead decreases due to instability. The other three either contradict the explanation (e.g., A misattributes the reason for investment decline to unmentioned initial costs), describe scenarios not discussed (e.g., B’s focus on dependency), or reverse the actual relationship (e.g., D claims investment increases with stabilization). That matches choice C — C is correct.
